result:
batch into single query: SELECT * FROM skus WHERE sku IN (...) — one connection regardless of cart size
3 SKUs or 47 SKUs, same cost pattern, faster than both loop and Promise.all for large carts
preserves common case (3 SKUs is tiny IN list, negligible overhead)
kills tail risk (47-SKU order no longer grabs 47 connections)
if SKU lookup has per-row logic that can't batch, fall back to bounded concurrency via p-limit at 5 — caps worst case at 5 connections per request, still 10x faster than sequential for big carts
prefer batch; reach for p-limit only if batch query isn't feasible
